title: TPU v1 · Encoding a Tiled-GEMM Controller as a Two-Process FSM
oneLine: FSM RTL separates state storage from next-state and output logic. A TPU-like tiled GEMM follows Load Weights → Stream Activations → Accumulate → Write Back; this makes a clear two-process example, with always_ff updating current state and always_comb computing transitions and control outputs. The specific TPU controller RTL is not public.

legend.name: States (rounded boxes from the design diagram)
legend.desc: Idle, LoadW, Stream, Accumulate, and WriteBack represent current state. RTL encodes them in state_reg using a typed enum or binary/one-hot bits. State does not perform work itself; it selects the control actions for the current cycle.

legend.name: Transitions (edges and arrows)
legend.desc: Each arrow states the condition for moving from one state to another. In RTL, case(state) branches assign next_state from handshake or counter conditions; when no condition is met, next_state remains state.

legend.name: State register (always_ff with <=)
legend.desc: The first FSM process is the only clocked state storage. On each rising edge it captures next_state; reset returns it to Idle. The small triangle denotes clocked flops.

legend.name: Next-state/output logic (always_comb with defaults)
legend.desc: The second process is purely combinational. It reads state, computes next_state, and decodes control outputs. Assign next_state=state and default all outputs before the case statement, then include a default branch to prevent unintended latches and provide recovery.

legend.name: Control outputs (decoded enables)
legend.desc: The FSM drives datapath controls: load_w_en loads weights, stream_en injects activations, acc_en enables 32-bit accumulation, and wb_en writes results from the 4 MiB accumulators to the 24 MiB Unified Buffer.

steps.cap: ① Idle: after reset, all enables are low and the array waits. When the host sends a compact command over PCIe, combinational logic points next_state to LoadW.
② LoadW: on entry, load_w_en fills the 256 × 256 array with a weight tile for weight-stationary execution. When the load counter completes, next_state becomes Stream.

③ Stream and Accumulate: stream_en supplies activations from the Unified Buffer each cycle while acc_en accumulates partial sums. These phases may overlap as a steady-state pipeline until the tile completes.
④ WriteBack: after streaming completes, wb_en writes results from the 4 MiB accumulator storage into the 24 MiB Unified Buffer. Every state transition occurs when always_ff captures next_state.

⑤ Loop: after writeback, combinational logic returns next_state to Idle or proceeds directly to the next tile. One state register plus complete combinational logic implements the entire transition diagram.

before: Select the language and state encoding: use a SystemVerilog enum or equivalent and choose binary or one-hot encoding. Five states fit in three binary bits; one-hot may reduce decode depth at the cost of more flops. Prioritize a reviewed, readable policy.
Define reset behavior: choose polarity and assertion/release policy consistently. The FSM must reach a known initial state—Idle—after reset; an X state at power-up is unacceptable.

Define inputs, outputs, and widths: list host start/command signals, per-phase counters or done handshakes, and load_w_en, stream_en, acc_en, and wb_en with polarity and timing. This port table drives the case logic.

Constrain coding style: clocked state uses nonblocking <=, combinational logic uses blocking =, one signal has one owner, and no combinational path may leave an output unassigned. Enforce these rules with lint.
Define success criteria: cover the complete Idle→LoadW→Stream→Accumulate→WriteBack→Idle path, prove reset-to-Idle, and require zero unintended latches or multiple drivers after lint and synthesis.
during: Use a two- or three-process FSM rather than mixing concerns: one always_ff process stores current state; one always_comb process computes next state and outputs. This separation makes timing, review, and modification clearer than a monolithic clocked controller.
Keep the sequential process minimal: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if(rst) state<=Idle; else state<=next_state; end. It only captures next_state using nonblocking assignment.

Assign combinational defaults first: begin with next_state = state and set every control output inactive, then override only what changes in case(state). Without complete assignment, synthesis must preserve a prior value and infers a latch.

Include a default case: even when an enum lists legal values, an upset or invalid one-hot code can occur. A default transition to Idle or another defined safe state supplies recovery and guards completeness.

Choose Moore versus Mealy outputs deliberately: Moore enables derived only from state are stable and easier to time; Mealy outputs can respond in the same cycle but may create longer combinational paths or glitches. Array-control enables generally favor Moore behavior.

State the evidence boundary: TPU v1 discloses a tiled GEMM datapath and host-controlled compact instruction set, but not controller state names or RTL. The states and enables here are an instructional implementation of the known load/stream/accumulate/writeback behavior.

result.label: Coding style
result.value: Two-process FSM: clocked state + combinational next-state/output logic

result.label: Sequential process (example)
result.value: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(rst) state<=Idle; else state<=next_state;

result.label: Combinational process (example)
result.value: always_comb begin next=state; ... case(state) ... default: next=Idle; end

result.label: Latch-prevention rule
result.value: Assign defaults first and include a case default

result.label: External controls
result.value: load_w_en / stream_en / acc_en / wb_en, decoded from current state

after: Run FSM lint: check for latches, multiple drivers, assignment-style violations, incomplete case behavior, and missing defaults before simulation.
Cover every state and transition: drive the complete loop, stalled conditions that remain in state, and every exit condition. Use state and transition coverage to find dead or unreachable behavior.
Verify reset and illegal-state recovery: prove reset reaches Idle with no X state. For one-hot encoding, inject illegal codes and confirm deterministic recovery through default handling.
Run CDC checks: if FSM inputs come from PCIe, core, or DDR3 clock domains, ensure each is synchronized or handshaken correctly before being sampled.

Run trial synthesis and hand off: confirm only flops and combinational logic are inferred, no latches exist, and the controller meets the target clock. Freeze state definitions, transition tables, and output timing for verification.
source: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a TPU, Jouppi et al., ISCA 2017 (the tiled GEMM flow, weight-stationary execution, 24 MiB Unified Buffer, 4 MiB accumulators, and host-issued compact CISC commands are disclosed; the two-process FSM, defaults, state names, enables, and HDL are standard illustrative RTL, not published TPU controller source)
